believe that God is able? Do we really believe he's developing us and at the same time he's
working so that he would receive all glory? Because it's God that did it. It's God that
fed those 5,000 people, not that lad's lunch. The lad's lunch was made available but God
blessed it and multiplied it so they could all eat. I don't know what God's doing in
your life. I don't know if you've ever experienced it in your life. But it's a good thing when
God brings us to a place. We know we're exactly where God wants us to be. And something happens.
We don't retreat in misseeing what God's going to do. We say, God, I know you led me here.
God, I know that you directed me. I know I've been obedient to you. Not pridefully but I
just know you led me here. And so God, you knew all about this and I'm going to trust
you to do what only you can do. Proved it, Meribah. Let's pray. Father, we love you.
Thank you so much for loving us. Thank you for this psalm and the way that the children
of Israel, every time they would observe for generations of people, observing the Feast
of Tabernacles, they would get there to verse number 7, ending with the word selah to pause
to meditate to consider that it was there at that place, Meribah, that God proved them.
What a great reminder. Generations could ask the question, what is that? You could go back
and rehearse the history of what you did in providing water from a rock. And God, I pray
you'd help us to trust you. God, help us to be faithful and to remain in the place where
you've called us to be, to see you do what only you can do. I pray that you'd bless
the time of prayer again. We thank you that you hear an answer. I pray that you'd be glorified
